questions.q: What was Nala famous for building in the Ramayana?
options: A bridge across the ocean to Lanka
questions.explanation: Nala is celebrated as the vanara who engineered the great causeway across the sea to Lanka.

questions.q: To which race of beings did Nala belong?
questions.explanation: Nala was a vanara, one of the monkey-folk who served in Rama's army.

questions.q: The bridge Nala built led across the sea to which island?
questions.explanation: The causeway spanned the ocean to Lanka, the island stronghold of Ravana.

questions.q: Whose army was Nala a part of when he built the bridge?
questions.explanation: Nala served in Rama's vanara host that marched against Ravana.

questions.q: What special skill is Nala best known for?
questions.explanation: Nala is the vanara architect, renowned for his engineering skill in constructing the causeway.

questions.q: What materials did the vanaras use to build Nala's bridge?
questions.explanation: The vanara host bridged the sea with great rocks, boulders, and felled trees.

questions.q: Nala was the son of which kind of figure?
questions.explanation: Nala was the son of a divine craftsman, from whom he inherited his building talent.

questions.q: Why did Rama's army need a bridge in the first place?
options: To cross the ocean separating them from Lanka
questions.explanation: The vast ocean lay between the army on the mainland and Ravana's island of Lanka.

questions.q: The bridge built by Nala is traditionally known by which name?
questions.explanation: The causeway is famously called Rama Setu, the bridge of Rama.

questions.q: What was the purpose of Rama crossing the bridge to Lanka?
questions.explanation: Rama crossed to Lanka to wage war on Ravana and rescue his abducted wife, Sita.

questions.q: According to popular tradition, why did the stones used in the bridge float on the water?
options: They were made of light pumice
The name of Rama was written upon them
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that stones inscribed with the name of Rama floated upon the sea.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, who was the deity of the sea that the army first sought to appease before building?
questions.explanation: Rama appealed to the lord of the ocean, Samudra (Varuna), who then advised that Nala should build the bridge.

questions.q: Which divine craftsman is named in tradition as Nala's father?
questions.explanation: Nala is traditionally said to be the son of Vishwakarma, the architect of the gods, which explains his skill.

questions.q: How long did it take the vanaras to complete the bridge, according to the Valmiki Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Valmiki's Ramayana states the causeway was completed in five days by the vanara host under Nala.

questions.q: Which vanara is often paired with Nala as a fellow builder of the bridge in later tradition?
questions.explanation: In many retellings Nila is named alongside Nala as a co-builder of the great causeway.

questions.q: The bridge enabled which major phase of the epic to begin?
options: The siege and war at Lanka
questions.explanation: Once the army crossed, the war against Ravana and the siege of Lanka commenced.

questions.q: Roughly how long was the bridge said to be in the Valmiki Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Valmiki describes the causeway as a hundred yojanas long and ten yojanas wide.

questions.q: Which event immediately followed the army's crossing of the bridge?
options: The encampment before Lanka and the war
The burning of Lanka by Hanuman
questions.explanation: After crossing, Rama's host encamped before Lanka, leading into the great battle.

questions.q: Hanuman had already reached Lanka before the bridge was built. How did he do so?
questions.explanation: Hanuman crossed by his mighty leap over the sea; the bridge was needed only for the whole army.

questions.q: Which righteous brother of Ravana joined Rama before the bridge was built?
questions.explanation: Vibhishana, Ravana's virtuous brother, defected to Rama and aided the army at the shore.

questions.q: What is the modern geographic feature traditionally identified with Nala's bridge?
options: The Palk Strait chain of shoals between India and Sri Lanka
questions.explanation: The chain of limestone shoals between India and Sri Lanka, called Adam's Bridge, is identified with Rama Setu.

questions.q: The vanaras gathered the boulders and trees for the bridge mainly from where?
questions.explanation: The vanaras uprooted trees and tore rocks from the surrounding mountains and forests to cast into the sea.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, the ocean-lord advised that Nala could build the bridge because of which fact about Nala?
options: He was a son of the divine architect Vishwakarma and shared his power
questions.explanation: Samudra revealed that Nala, son of Vishwakarma, possessed his father's creative power and could span the sea.

questions.q: What did Rama do that finally moved the ocean-deity to appear and offer help?
options: He threatened to dry up the sea with a fiery weapon
He performed a hundred horse sacrifices
He prayed silently for a single night
questions.explanation: When the sea ignored him, Rama took up a blazing arrow to dry the ocean, and the terrified Samudra then appeared.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, how wide was the causeway said to be?
questions.explanation: Valmiki gives the bridge's dimensions as a hundred yojanas long and ten yojanas wide.

questions.q: According to the Valmiki Ramayana, how much of the bridge did the mighty vanaras complete on the first day?
questions.explanation: Valmiki records that fourteen yojanas of the bridge were raised on the first day of work.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, when Rama directed his blazing weapon, the ocean-lord asked him to instead release the arrow upon which region of evil-doers?
options: A northern region called Drumakulya inhabited by sinful dwellers
questions.explanation: Samudra asked Rama to loose the arrow on Drumakulya in the north, whose wicked inhabitants troubled the sea.

questions.q: Which weapon or missile was Rama about to use against the sea before Samudra relented?
questions.explanation: Rama fitted a fearsome, blazing arrow of the Brahma weapon to his bow to dry the sea before Samudra yielded.

questions.q: After the war, the Pushpaka Vimana, not Nala's bridge, was used for what journey?
options: Carrying Rama and Sita back home to Ayodhya
Bringing the vanara army to Lanka
Ferrying Rama across the Ganga in exile
questions.explanation: The flying chariot Pushpaka bore Rama, Sita, and their companions back to Ayodhya after Ravana's defeat.

questions.q: Which of these statements about Nala's role in the epic is accurate?
options: He slew Ravana in single combat
He was the chief architect who directed the building of the sea-bridge
He drew the Lakshman Rekha around Sita's hut
questions.explanation: Nala's defining role is as the architect who led the construction of the causeway to Lanka.
